danger_signals.visual_form: no visible life bar or health indicator; player character sprite removal on contact
danger_signals.meaning: all enemies and environmental hazards present lethal threat; any collision is terminal danger

danger_signals.visual_form: sprite-based enemies appearing in scrolling platformer space
danger_signals.meaning: forward progress requires active threat avoidance and evasion

safety_signals.visual_form: gaps between enemies; platform-to-platform progression paths without active threat sprites
safety_signals.meaning: safe traversal windows exist between danger zones
level terrain itself does not directly kill player (danger is externalized to enemies)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: ninja/shinobi visual archetype in protagonist sprite

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: varied enemy sprite designs indicating different enemy types and hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemies present in current screen/level segment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must avoid collision; forward progression blocked until enemies are cleared or bypassed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: platformer screen layout with progressive enemy placement across horizontal space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: side-scrolling movement required; cannot return to previous sections; unidirectional forced progression

environmental_storytelling.example: enemy density and placement increases as player progresses through side-scrolling stages, communicating escalating opposition
different enemy visual designs across levels imply different faction units or threat types without explicit narrative exposition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: controllable traversal across platforms; evasion/jumping responses to threat proximity
sprite removal; instant level restart or continue state
predictable movement patterns across platforms; visual positioning communicates threat zones

notes: Ninja Crusaders uses minimal narrative exposition; visual communication relies entirely on sprite design, platformer level layout, and the one-hit-death mechanic to convey danger/safety states. Faction identity is suggested through character archetype (ninja protagonist) but is not explicitly visualized through environmental markers or symbolic systems. The game employs pure mechanical difficulty as world-state communication rather than explicit visual faction indicators. Source material is limited to technical metadata and does not contain detailed gameplay documentation.
